column1: What are the three core pillars of the Google Manager Framework?
column2: Build Community, Deliver Results, Develop People. (From the 'Create a High-Performing Team' course)

column1: When a project fails, what should a manager focus on identifying instead of who made the mistake?
column2: What the system lacked that allowed the mistake. (Shifting from a blame culture to a learning culture)

column1: What is the core principle of Radical Candor?
column2: Challenging directly while caring personally. (Useful for addressing difficult but capable team members)

column1: What three critical ingredients must a manager balance to drive high performance?
What three competing needs must be balanced when allocating work (the "three-legged stool")?
column2: Individual Skills/Strengths, Individual Interests/Growth, and Business Needs/Deadlines.

column1: What is the recommended focus shift for 1:1 check-ins?
What does SBI stand for in the context of providing structured feedback?
column2: Situation, Behavior, Impact. (Ensures feedback is objective and tied to specific outcomes)
